text.title: Agentic RAG Iterative Retrieval Core Rules
text.summary: 4 testable rules + 2 terminal conclusions every iterative-retrieval implementation must enforce.

rule.statement: Loop is capped at `max_iterations` (default 3, hard cap 5). On cap reached without sufficient verdict, log + route to human review.
rule.rationale: Unbounded loops diverge and burn unlimited API budget; hard cap keeps latency + cost predictable.

rule.statement: Sufficiency judge runs on Haiku or Sonnet. Opus reserved for final answer synthesis.
rule.rationale: Yes/no judgement does not need reasoning depth; Haiku reduces per-iter cost ~10x vs Opus.

rule.statement: Chunks deduplicated across iterations by `chunk_id` before passing to judge or generator.
rule.rationale: Duplicates inflate token usage and bias the sufficiency judge.

rule.statement: cosine_sim(original_query_emb, refined_query_emb) >= 0.7 else reset refined query to original.
rule.rationale: After 2+ iterations refinement drifts; the refined query answers a different question.

rule.statement: Multi-hop question class AND latency ≥3s AND distinct judge+generator models → wire iterative retriever.
Single-hop OR latency <2s OR untrusted corpus without sanitisation → skip; use single-pass RAG.
